skill.name: tests-maintenance
skill.description: Guides focused maintenance of the IdeaVim test suite, including disabled tests, Neovim exclusions, annotation documentation, and readable test content.
skill.summary: Maintains IdeaVim test quality through focused review and cleanup workflows.
skill.author: JetBrains

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command and blocker patterns in SKILL.md. Review found the high blocker, reconnaissance, and weak cryptography hits are false positives from prose, Markdown, and annotation names. The legitimate residual risk is medium because the skill instructs agents to run local find, grep, and Gradle commands inside a repository.

medium_findings.title: Repository Command Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill directs agents to run local shell and Gradle commands for test discovery and execution. These commands match the skill purpose, but Gradle can execute repository build logic and should only be used in trusted working trees.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell and Gradle commands are explicit and relevant to test maintenance. The risk is contextual rather than malicious because no exfiltration, obfuscation, or unauthorized network behavior is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Matches
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography findings are not cryptographic operations. The referenced lines contain metadata, Markdown, annotation fields, and ordinary text such as descriptions and expected test output.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no hashing, encryption, or algorithm selection logic in the cited content. The matches come from non-code text and annotation examples.

low_findings.title: False Positive Reconnaissance Matches
low_findings.description: The system and network reconnaissance findings are ordinary review instructions about checking test reasons and Neovim-related behavior. No command enumerates hosts, networks, users, or system secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe test quality checks and annotation maintenance. There is no evidence of network probing or host reconnaissance intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes find, grep, and Gradle command examples. They are legitimate project maintenance commands, but agents should treat repository-local build execution as potentially unsafe in untrusted projects.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly present and executable. They are not inherently malicious, but they require trust in the checked-out project and local build tooling.

content.value_statement: Large test suites collect disabled tests, unclear annotations, and low-value fixtures over time. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a focused workflow for small, reviewable IdeaVim test maintenance changes.
actual_capabilities: Selects a small subset of IdeaVim test files for focused maintenance.
Reviews disabled Kotlin tests and checks whether they can be re-enabled.
Audits TestWithoutNeovim annotations for clear skip reasons and descriptions.
Improves test readability by replacing meaningless fixture text.
Checks VimBehaviorDiffers annotations for current and documented behavior differences.
Suggests concise commit and pull request descriptions for test-only changes.
limitations: It is specific to the IdeaVim repository layout and test conventions.
It does not fix production code bugs or implement missing features.
It depends on local Gradle and test environment availability.
It may require human judgment for behavior differences between IdeaVim and Vim.

use_cases.title: Review Disabled Tests
use_cases.description: Check whether disabled IdeaVim tests now pass and document or remove obsolete exclusions.

use_cases.title: Document Neovim Exclusions
use_cases.description: Find TestWithoutNeovim annotations that need clearer reasons or descriptions.
use_cases.target_user: Contributors working on Vim compatibility tests

use_cases.title: Improve Test Readability
use_cases.description: Replace unclear fixture strings with realistic content that explains the tested behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Find a Maintenance Target
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for choosing one test file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Select one IdeaVim test file for maintenance. Explain why it is a good small target before making changes.

prompt_templates.title: Review Disabled Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Find disabled tests in the selected file. Run only the relevant tests and recommend whether each should remain disabled, be documented, or be re-enabled.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Neovim Exclusions
prompt_templates.prompt: Review TestWithoutNeovim annotations in this area. Check whether each reason is still valid and whether non-obvious cases need descriptions.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Test Maintenance PR
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for a complete cleanup change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a small IdeaVim test maintenance pass. Limit the scope, run targeted tests, summarize evidence, and draft a pull request title and body.

output_examples.input: Review one disabled test class for possible cleanup.
output: Selected a single test class with disabled tests.
Found one test that now passes and one that still fails.
Recommended re-enabling the passing test and adding a clearer reason to the remaining disabled test.

output_examples.input: Check Neovim exclusion annotations in a test file.
output: Identified annotations with clear reasons and no required changes.
Flagged one non-obvious exclusion that needs a description.
Suggested a short explanation tied to viewport behavior differences.

output_examples.input: Improve unreadable fixture text in a motion test.
output: Replaced placeholder text with realistic sample content.
Recommended a targeted test run for the edited class.

best_practices: Keep each maintenance pass small and focused on one file or narrow area.
Run targeted tests before changing disabled annotations or behavior documentation.
Explain every changed exclusion with the current IdeaVim and Vim behavior context.
anti_patterns: Do not change production code while using this skill.
Do not remove disabled tests without verifying that the covered feature is obsolete.
Do not replace unclear test data with equally generic placeholder strings.

faq.question: What repository is this skill designed for?
faq.answer: It is designed for the IdeaVim repository and its Kotlin test suite conventions.

faq.question: Can it fix failing production behavior?
faq.answer: No. The skill is limited to test maintenance and documentation cleanup.

faq.question: Does it run tests automatically?
faq.answer: It provides commands and workflow guidance. The agent still needs an available local Gradle environment.

faq.question: Why does it mention Neovim?
faq.answer: IdeaVim compares some behavior against Neovim, so exclusions need clear reasons.

faq.question: Is this useful for new contributors?
faq.answer: Yes. It helps new contributors choose small, reviewable test cleanup tasks.

faq.question: What should reviewers check after using it?
faq.answer: Reviewers should confirm test scope, annotation reasons, and targeted test results.
